items.title: Regeneron basks in first gene therapy approval as Dupixent, Eylea carry robust Q1 earnings
items.snippet: Regeneron hauled in $3.6 billion during the first quarter of 2026, as analysts homed in on a slight Eylea HD miss and key upcoming readouts, including for LAG3 candidate fianlimab in metastatic melanoma.

items.title: Biogen ready to catch Alzheimer’s patients transitioning off Lilly’s Kisunla
items.snippet: Key dosing differences between Eli Lilly’s Kisunla and Biogen’s Leqembi are about to come to a head in the Alzheimer's market as patients end their 18-month course of Lilly’s product.

items.title: Chiesi snaps up KalVista for $1.9B, betting big on rare disease med Ekterly
items.snippet: Chiesi Group is taking KalVista Pharmaceuticals under its wing, paying $1.9 billion for the biotech’s oral therapy Ekterly to treat severe swelling episodes caused by the rare genetic disorder hereditary angioedema.

items.title: AstraZeneca bullish on $80B sales goal after beating expectations, Phase 3 data wins
items.snippet: After a quarter in which sales topped $15 billion and key readouts went AstraZeneca’s way, the company is increasingly confident that its 2030 revenue target is in reach.

items.title: GSK’s Shingrix strategy shift drives sales beat even as vaccines slow
items.snippet: While sales for most of GSK’s shots slumped as vaccine skepticism continues to climb in the U.S., Shingrix jumped 20% to almost $1.4 billion in the first quarter, emerging as the pharma’s top-selling product.

items.title: Pfizer settles with generic drugmakers to protect blockbuster drug until 2031
items.snippet: The settlements delay the entry of generic copies of Pfizer’s Vyndamax by almost three years, stabilizing sales of a drug that generated $3.8 billion in the U.S. last year.

items.title: AstraZeneca faces uphill battle at upcoming adcomm in face of FDA questions
items.snippet: In briefing documents released Wednesday, the FDA raises doubts about two AstraZeneca assets set to be discussed Friday at the agency’s first drug-related advisory committee meeting in nine months.

items.title: Generic of Novo’s GLP-1 arrives in Canada, a ‘test case for the world’
items.snippet: Analysts will be watching as a generic version of semaglutide—marketed by Novo Nordisk as Wegovy for weight loss—launches in Canada as a test case for future price erosion in the U.S.

items.title: Amgen, AstraZeneca join FDA effort harnessing cloud for real-time clinical trials
items.snippet: The FDA’s real-time clinical trial mechanism allows drug sponsors to transmit data immediately to the regulator through the cloud—a system that could “compress drug development timelines,” Jefferies analysts said.

items.title: Novartis, PTC push Huntington’s drug into Phase 3, mum on accelerated approval
items.snippet: Phase 2 data from PTC Therapeutics showed that the Novartis-partnered Huntington’s disease asset slowed progression by more than 50%. Analysts say the decision to initiate a last-stage trial reflects a lack of confidence in an accelerated FDA nod.

items.title: Q1 earnings take off, Lilly strikes many deals, Regeneron notches historic approval, FDA raises questions
items.snippet: Sanofi and Novartis kick off the heart of earnings season; Lilly strikes its fourth pact in as many weeks; Regeneron earns landmark approval for a gene therapy for a type of genetic deafness, and also strikes a White House deal; FDA asks Amgen to withdraw Tavneos and, separately, issues Commissioner’s National Priorit...

items.title: Biotech angels lean into deeper diligence and disciplined early bets
items.snippet: Angel investors are raising the bar with tighter criteria, backing strong teams, realistic markets and clear paths to exit while applying deeper, domain-specific diligence to early stage bets.

items.title: Altitude Labs serves up higher education for biotech founders
items.snippet: Altitude Labs, an offshoot of AI-focused techbio Recursion, is teaching scientists to build companies, one founder at a time.

items.title: Lilly can’t stop the dealing, with nearly $21B spent on M&A this year—so far
items.snippet: With six acquisitions already this year, Eli Lilly’s business development shows no signs of stopping as executives make good on a promise to spend their GLP-1 gains.

items.title: Patient advocate offers Recursion a powerful reason to keep going in rare disease
items.snippet: Staff at Salt Lake City-based techbio company Recursion recently heard from Jenny and Tim Jones about their challenging family history of familial adenomatous polyposis.

items.title: AbbVie sinks talons into KRAS with right to buy Kestrel for up to $1.45B
items.snippet: AbbVie is setting up a shot to buy Kestrel Therapeutics down the line, as the biotech doses patients in a Phase 1 trial for the oral pan-KRAS inhibitor KST-6051 in solid tumors.

items.title: Boehringer, Zealand’s obesity drug ‘more akin’ to Novo’s Wegovy in Phase 3
items.snippet: Topline Phase 3 results of survodutide—licensed to Boehringer Ingelheim from Zealand Pharma—are more comparable to Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy than to Eli Lilly’s Zepbound, William Blair analysts said on Tuesday.

items.title: AACR 2026: Combo therapies by Moderna, Marengo show promise in skin, breast cancer
items.snippet: Cancer cocktails pairing Moderna’s mRNA-4359 with Merck’s Keytruda and Marengo’s invikafusp alfa with Gilead Sciences’ Trodelvy showed promising results, while a complex combination by Agenus and MiNK Therapeutics failed to elicit an overall response.

items.title: Novartis cuts hit New Jersey again, affecting 60 employees
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Lilly, AI biotech Profluent ink $2.25B pact in search of genetic medicine ‘holy grail’
items.snippet: Weight loss giant Eli Lilly has penned a DNA editing deal with Profluent Bio, continuing its dealmaking spree as well as its commitment to artificial intelligence.

items.title: BioSpace elevates journalism as biopharma evolves in the Trump era
items.snippet: Neal and Azbee awards have validated our approach to reporting on the industry at a time of unprecedented shifts at the FDA and other federal agencies.

items.title: Biotech has become a good news story. Let the sun shine
items.snippet: Doubling survival in pancreatic cancer, a long-fought rare disease approval, a massive IPO and ambitious biotech entrepreneurs have BioSpace Senior Editor Annalee Armstrong feeling upbeat about the biotech scene.

items.title: Replimune cries foul on regulatory flexibility. But many Americans want a stricter FDA
items.snippet: After Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug was rejected for a second time, CEO Sushil Patel slammed the FDA for failing to exercise regulatory flexibility, while other experts bemoaned the agency’s lack of consistency. With new safety guidelines for gene editing therapies, the FDA has taken a first step toward fixing bo...

items.title: With oral obesity race underway, all eyes are on Lilly’s Foundayo launch
items.snippet: Novo Nordisk’s oral Wegovy has a few months’ head start on Eli Lilly’s newly approved pill. While the Indianapolis pharma has come from behind the Danish rival in the weight loss space before, last time it clearly had the better drug.
